revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 河津八幡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高624 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 耳高…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 河津八幡神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高624 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 耳高大明神・多祁伊志豆岐命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = [[天平]]5年（[[733年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 耳高神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月22日-23日 |神事 = }} '''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高（みだか）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、[[今井浜海岸駅]]・[[国道135号]]至近の[[今井浜海水浴場|今井浜海岸]]の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。
創建は[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）と伝承され、[[式内社]]である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の[[歌舞伎]]役者・[[市川小團次 (4代目)|4代目市川小團次]]とその養子・[[市川左團次 (初代)|初代市川左團次]]が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される[[式三番]]（[[三番叟]]）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）
== 文化財 == * 見高(みたか)[[式三番|式三番叟]]([[三番叟]]) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:みたかしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Expand Japanese|見高神社}}

{{Infobox religious building

| native_name = 見高神社
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 3 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 見高神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高624 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 耳高大明神・多祁伊志豆岐命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = [[天平]]5年（[[733年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 耳高神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月22日-23日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=見高神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高（みだか）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、[[今井浜海岸駅]]・[[国道135号]]至近の[[今井浜海水浴場|今井浜海岸]]の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。
創建は[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）と伝承され、[[式内社]]である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の[[歌舞伎]]役者・[[市川小團次 (4代目)|4代目市川小團次]]とその養子・[[市川左團次 (初代)|初代市川左團次]]が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される[[式三番]]（[[三番叟]]）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）
== 文化財 == * 見高(みたか)[[式三番|式三番叟]]([[三番叟]]) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:みたかしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mitaka Shrine | native_name = 見高神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Mitaka Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.75325|139.012667}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].
== instance of (P31) ==

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 見高神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高624 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 耳高大明神・多祁伊志豆岐命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = [[天平]]5年（[[733年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 耳高神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月22日-23日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=見高神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高（みだか）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、[[今井浜海岸駅]]・[[国道135号]]至近の[[今井浜海水浴場|今井浜海岸]]の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。
創建は[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）と伝承され、[[式内社]]である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の[[歌舞伎]]役者・[[市川小團次 (4代目)|4代目市川小團次]]とその養子・[[市川左團次 (初代)|初代市川左團次]]が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される[[式三番]]（[[三番叟]]）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）
== 文化財 == * 見高(みたか)[[式三番|式三番叟]]([[三番叟]]) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:みたかしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 見高神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高624 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 耳高大明神・多祁伊志豆岐命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]]論社 |創建 = [[天平]]5年（[[733年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 耳高神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月22日-23日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=見高神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[河津町]]見高（みだか）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、[[今井浜海岸駅]]・[[国道135号]]至近の[[今井浜海水浴場|今井浜海岸]]の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。
創建は[[天平]]5年（[[733年]]）と伝承され、[[式内社]]である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の[[歌舞伎]]役者・[[市川小團次 (4代目)|4代目市川小團次]]とその養子・[[市川左團次 (初代)|初代市川左團次]]が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される[[式三番]]（[[三番叟]]）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）
== 文化財 == * 見高(みたか)[[式三番|式三番叟]]([[三番叟]]) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 見高神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高624 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 耳高大明神・多祁伊志豆岐命 |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社 |創建 = {{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}） |本殿 = |別名 = 耳高神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月22日-23日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=見高神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高（みだか）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、{{jalink|今井浜海岸駅|Q6002975}}・{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}至近の{{jalink|今井浜海水浴場|Q11376648|今井浜海岸}}の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。
創建は{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）と伝承され、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。
牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の{{jalink|歌舞伎|Q199701}}役者・{{jalink|市川小團次 (4代目)|Q11480242|4代目市川小團次}}とその養子・{{jalink|市川左團次 (初代)|Q11480254|初代市川左團次}}が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される{{jalink|式三番|Q11254805}}（{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）
== 文化財 == * 見高(みたか){{jalink|式三番|Q11254805|式三番叟}}({{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:河津町の歴史]] [[Category:河津町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

[[JA:見高神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 見高神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高624
|位置   = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 耳高大明神・多祁伊志豆岐命
|社格   = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社
|創建   = {{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 耳高神社
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 10月22日-23日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=見高神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高（みだか）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、{{jalink|今井浜海岸駅|Q6002975}}・{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}至近の{{jalink|今井浜海水浴場|Q11376648|今井浜海岸}}の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）と伝承され、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の{{jalink|歌舞伎|Q199701}}役者・{{jalink|市川小團次 (4代目)|Q11480242|4代目市川小團次}}とその養子・{{jalink|市川左團次 (初代)|Q11480254|初代市川左團次}}が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される{{jalink|式三番|Q11254805}}（{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）

=== 文化財 ===
* 見高(みたか){{jalink|式三番|Q11254805|式三番叟}}({{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 見高神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}}

|本殿   =

|札所等 =

|神事   =

'''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高（みだか）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、{{jalink|今井浜海岸駅|Q6002975}}・{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}至近の{{jalink|今井浜海水浴場|Q11376648|今井浜海岸}}の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）と伝承され、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の{{jalink|歌舞伎|Q199701}}役者・{{jalink|市川小團次 (4代目)|Q11480242|4代目市川小團次}}とその養子・{{jalink|市川左團次 (初代)|Q11480254|初代市川左團次}}が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される{{jalink|式三番|Q11254805}}（{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）

=== 文化財 ===
* 見高(みたか){{jalink|式三番|Q11254805|式三番叟}}({{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 見高神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}}

|社格   = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社
|創建   = {{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 耳高神社
|札所等 =

|神事   =

'''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高（みだか）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、{{jalink|今井浜海岸駅|Q6002975}}・{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}至近の{{jalink|今井浜海水浴場|Q11376648|今井浜海岸}}の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）と伝承され、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の{{jalink|歌舞伎|Q199701}}役者・{{jalink|市川小團次 (4代目)|Q11480242|4代目市川小團次}}とその養子・{{jalink|市川左團次 (初代)|Q11480254|初代市川左團次}}が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される{{jalink|式三番|Q11254805}}（{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）

=== 文化財 ===
* 見高(みたか){{jalink|式三番|Q11254805|式三番叟}}({{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。

{{nihongo|'''Mitaka Shrine'''|見高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 見高神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高624
|位置   = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|11.7|N|139|0|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 耳高大明神・多祁伊志豆岐命
|社格   = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}論社
|創建   = {{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 耳高神社
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 10月22日-23日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=見高神社|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''見高神社'''（みたかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|河津町|Q1203861}}見高（みだか）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 見高神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
河津町東端の見高（みだか）地区、その南部沿岸、{{jalink|今井浜海岸駅|Q6002975}}・{{jalink|国道135号|Q6156779}}至近の{{jalink|今井浜海水浴場|Q11376648|今井浜海岸}}の東隣り、下河津漁港や見高浜を臨む東方の山中、旧河津町立東小学校の南西に隣接する形で鎮座している。

創建は{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}5年（{{jalink|733年|Q23570}}）と伝承され、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''多祁伊志豆岐命神社'''（たけいしつきのみこと）の論社にもなっている<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ 見高神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">境内由緒書</ref>。

牛に乗った耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）の創建伝説に基づいて、10月22日-23日の例大祭では、「舌べら餅」をお供えする風習の他、当地出身の{{jalink|歌舞伎|Q199701}}役者・{{jalink|市川小團次 (4代目)|Q11480242|4代目市川小團次}}とその養子・{{jalink|市川左團次 (初代)|Q11480254|初代市川左團次}}が関与する形で形成された、境内の神楽殿で奉納される{{jalink|式三番|Q11254805}}（{{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}）の伝統があり、河津町の無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 見高神社] - 河津町観光協会</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ 見高式三番叟] - 静岡・浜松・伊豆情報局</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 耳高大明神（みみたかだいみょうじん）・多祁伊志豆岐命（たけいしつきのみこと）

=== 文化財 ===
* 見高(みたか){{jalink|式三番|Q11254805|式三番叟}}({{jalink|三番叟|Q11356796}}) - 町指定無形民俗文化財。

== Japanese content ==
{{Shrine
|Name   = Mitaka Shrine
|Image  = {{Image requested|cat=Kamo District}}

|Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}} Mitaka 624

|Enshrined deity = Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto
|Shrine rank = Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}, {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} candidate shrine
|Founded = {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 5 ({{jalink|733|Q23570}})
|Main hall =

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{jalink|Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975}}, {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}, and just east of {{jalink|Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 5 ({{jalink|733|Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{jalink|Kabuki|Q199701}} actors {{jalink|Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{jalink|Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{jalink|Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975}}, {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}, and just east of {{jalink|Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 5 ({{jalink|733|Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{jalink|Kabuki|Q199701}} actors {{jalink|Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{jalink|Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{jalink|Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975}}, {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}, and just east of {{jalink|Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 5 ({{jalink|733|Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{jalink|Kabuki|Q199701}} actors {{jalink|Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{jalink|Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{jalink|Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975}}, {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}, and just east of {{jalink|Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 5 ({{jalink|733|Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{jalink|Kabuki|Q199701}} actors {{jalink|Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{jalink|Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

{{Shrine

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{jalink|Kawazu Town|Q1203861}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{jalink|Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975}}, {{jalink|National Route 135|Q6156779}}, and just east of {{jalink|Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{jalink|Tenpyō era|Q1199879}} 5 ({{jalink|733|Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{jalink|Kabuki|Q199701}} actors {{jalink|Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{jalink|Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{jalink|Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{jalink|Sanbasō|Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975|1=Imaihama-Kaigan Station}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast|1=Imaihama Beach}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570|1=733}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji|1=Ichikawa Kodanji IV}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji|1=Ichikawa Sadanji I}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975|1=Imaihama-Kaigan Station}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast|1=Imaihama Beach}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570|1=733}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji|1=Ichikawa Kodanji IV}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji|1=Ichikawa Sadanji I}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975|1=Imaihama-Kaigan Station}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast|1=Imaihama Beach}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570|1=733}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji|1=Ichikawa Kodanji IV}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji|1=Ichikawa Sadanji I}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975|1=Imaihama-Kaigan Station}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast|1=Imaihama Beach}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570|1=733}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji|1=Ichikawa Kodanji IV}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji|1=Ichikawa Sadanji I}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

|Founded = {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570|1=733}})

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|WD=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|WD=Q6002975|1=Imaihama-Kaigan Station}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|WD=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|WD=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast|1=Imaihama Beach}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|WD=Q23570|1=733}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|WD=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|WD=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji|1=Ichikawa Kodanji IV}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|WD=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji|1=Ichikawa Sadanji I}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|WD=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|WD=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|qid=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|qid=Q6002975|1=Imaihama-Kaigan Station}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|qid=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|qid=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast|1=Imaihama Beach}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|qid=Q23570|1=733}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|qid=Q11254805|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|qid=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|qid=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|qid=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji|1=Ichikawa Kodanji IV}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|qid=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji|1=Ichikawa Sadanji I}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|qid=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|qid=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Mitaka Shrine''' (みたかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Mitaka, {{ill|lt=Kawazu Town|Q1203861|qid=Q1203861|1=Kawazu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401013 Mitaka Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
The shrine is situated in the easternmost Mitaka district of Kawazu Town, in the mountainous area to the east overlooking the Shimokawazu Fishing Port and Mitaka Beach. It stands adjacent to the southwest of the former Kawazu Town Higashi Elementary School, near {{ill|lt=Imaihama-Kaigan Station|Q6002975|qid=Q6002975|1=Imaihama-Kaigan Station}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 135|Q6156779|qid=Q6156779|1=Japan National Route 135}}, and just east of {{ill|lt=Imaihama Beach|Q11376648|qid=Q11376648|lt=Imaihama Coast|1=Imaihama Beach}}.

According to tradition, the shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō era|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=733|Q23570|qid=Q23570|1=733}}), and it is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki no Mikoto Shrine'''.<ref>[https://shrineheritager.com/mitaka-shrine/ Mitaka Shrine] – Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="yuisho">Shrine precinct origin inscription</ref>

Based on the founding legend of Mimitaka Daimyōjin riding on an ox, the grand annual festival held on October 22–23 includes the custom of offering “Shitabera-mochi.” In addition, there is a tradition of performing the {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|qid=Q11254805|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|qid=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}), dedicated in the shrine’s kagura hall. This tradition was formed with the involvement of the locally born {{ill|lt=Kabuki|Q199701|qid=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} actors {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Kodanji IV|Q11480242|qid=Q11480242|lt=4th Ichikawa Kodanji|1=Ichikawa Kodanji IV}} and his adopted son {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Sadanji I|Q11480254|qid=Q11480254|lt=1st Ichikawa Sadanji|1=Ichikawa Sadanji I}}, and it has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Kawazu Town.<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="kanko">[https://www.kawazu-onsen.com/sightseeing/41 Mitaka Shrine] – Kawazu Town Tourism Association</ref><ref name="joho">[https://shizuoka-hamamatsu-izu.com/event/mitakasikisannbasou/ Mitaka Shiki Sanbasō] – Shizuoka, Hamamatsu, Izu Information Bureau</ref>

=== Enshrined deities ===
* Mimitaka Daimyōjin · Takeishitsuki no Mikoto

=== Cultural properties ===
* Mitaka {{ill|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|Q11254805|qid=Q11254805|lt=Shiki Sanbasō|1=Shiki Sanban}} ({{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|qid=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}}) – Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
